key_insight: The study uses thermo-mechanical simulation to explain and compare how flash forms during inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy, linking process conditions to weld deformation behavior.
engineering_relevance: Its findings can help engineers tune welding parameters to improve joint quality and reduce defect risk in high-temperature superalloy components.
method: Thermo-mechanical simulation of inertia friction welding
Comparative screening of flash formation scenarios
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article in Welding in the World, but provides no quantitative results or validation details in the summary text.
watch_item: Simulation-based screening may not fully capture real weld variability, so experimental calibration and process-specific validation are important before deployment.
paper_title: Thermo-mechanical simulation and comparative screening of flash formation in inertia friction welding of FGH98 powder superalloy
